calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion For Order Granting Petition For Approval Of Settlement, Or Alternately, Motionfor Order Vacating And Setting Aside Order Granting Petition For Approval Of Settlement, Declarations Of Your H. Liu And Ross Mclauran Madden, Request For Judicial Notice, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross Madden for York Liu. Michael Freedman for James Liu.

Mr. Madden stated that he has read the tentative, that the fact it raises is that there was a misunderstanding, cited Mink vs. Superior Court, that a new fact can be something that was in the parties possession, that the agreement has characteristics that were unknown, that at the settlement conference they settled the partition matter only, that the addition of a recital changed everything and that change was not picked up by counsel or the client, cited Zamora vs. Claiborne stating that there are resemblances, that it was imprudent but not inexcusable, cited 473(b) that the partition matter was settled but that they never agreed to settle the San Bernardino matter, that there were additional recitals in the 3rd revision, that there can be a re-understanding of what you did not realize before, that something can be within your possession but not within knowledge and that Judge Lee's order is base on other sections of substantive law. The Court stated that if counsel did get the order approving vacated he would be arguing that the settlement agreement not be enforced and has already had the argument about reforming the agreement. Mr. Madden stated that the order is voidable as it was not served on Samuel Liu and that he believes the rubrik here is different. The Court stated that even if he was given the change to argue he would be back in front of Judge Lee. Mr. Freedman stated that the notice of entry of order was served on Samuel Liu, that there is no merit to this motion, that there are no new facts for reconsideration, that it was inexcusable neglect and that he did not receive timely notice on this hearing. The Court stated that it did not receive timely notice either. Mr. Madden stated that the settlement agreement itself is dependent upon this courts approval and that regarding the untimely notice he was not aware that tentative rulings were issued until Mr. Freedman emailed him at 5:08 p.m. The Court stated that the tentative ruling will stand, that it has considered the issues, that it does not think it is a new fact that could not have been raised at the time, that it is not appropriate for the court to reconsider or to relieve Mr. Liu of the relief that he asked for, that it is distinguishable from the other cases and that the court does not conclude that it is excusable. The Court denied the Motion for Reconsideration of the order approving the settlement agreement and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chu. Court Reporter: Heather Sturgis, CSR #11570, 800-288-3376 (Not Reported)

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of York H. Liu Trustee And Teyun Tsao And Appointment Of James H. Liu Successor Trustee; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And Surcharge
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross Madden for and with York Liu. Michael Freedman for James Liu.

Mr. Freedman stated that mediation was unsuccessful, asked that the Trustee be removed, stated that he received some documents and that the insurance on the 631-633 property is only an owner occupied policy. Mr. Freedman also stated that the property is in horrible/poor condition, that no one should be living in the property, that his Client is prepared to pay to relocate the tenants and that the Trust does not have the resources to pay for relocation. Counsel also spoke of the pending law suits and counter suits. Mr. Madden stated that his Client does not want to sell the property and that he wants to keep it for his Brother Samuel. The Court stated that it is not inclined to remove York, that the Parties need to find a solution and that the solution is a partition action. The Petition for Instructions and the Petition for Suspension of Powers and Removal York Liu Trustee, etc. were referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Jones. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-16-2013 Continued To Mar-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for James Liu. Ross Madden for and with York Liu (Respondent).

Mr. Madden stated that they have valuations for the properties, that both Parties have offered to be the Buyer and spoke of other civil pending matters. Mr. Freedman stated that he wants an order compelling an accounting, and spoke of actions taken by York and the condition of the properties. The Court was prepared to refer the matter out for trial to the Department of the Presiding Judge. The Parties elected/agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 3, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 3, 2014. The Petition for Instructions was continued to and the Parties are to return on March 10, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien. (Not Reported)

Counsel reported that they have discussed settlement, and stated that they need to iron out some issues with regard to the amount that is being offered for the buyout and the tenants living residing in the property. The Petition for Instructions was continued to December 16, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Parties have not settled, the matter will be set for trial.

Judge: Anne Bouliane. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial (Second Day)
Off Calendar May-17-2013 Vacated

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-10-2013 Advanced From May-10-13 To Apr-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Continued To Jun-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for James Liu. Ross Madden for and with York Liu (Respondent).

Mr. Freedman stated that the Trust has one remaining piece of real property, that the only issue remaining is to distribute the property in kind, and that there are remaining issues (accounting, Father's ashes, attorney fees) but that they are trying to bring administration of the Trust to an end. Mr. Madden argued that the Trust can not be resolved until the petition in Southern California to have $600,000.00 returned (investment matter) and the wrongful death action in San Francisco are resolved. Mr. Freedman's response was that the Trust is only funded by the two pieces of real property, that they will waive the accounting and that they are unable to resolve the issue of the ashes because James scattered the ashes. Counsel was asked to step out of the courtroom to meet and confer. Upon returning, Mr. Madden proposed that they be allowed a continuance so that they can continue working on an agreement. The Petition for Instructions was continued to June 5, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-24-2013 Continued To May-08-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Continued To May-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Russ Madden for York Liu ( appearing at 9:00 a.m.).

Mr. Madden reported that accounting was provided and requests that the matter be set for trial. The Court asks Mr. Madden to contact Mr. Freedman to see if he is coming to the hearing. Mr. Madden stated that he spoke to Mr. Freedman's office but they were unable to locate him. The Court tentatively sets trial date on May 10, 2013 and May 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 before Judge Sing with the understanding that it can be rescheduled.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

** After the conclusion of this hearing an apperance was made by Michael Freedman for James Liu (appearing at 10:30 a.m.).

Mr. Freedman reported that the tentative ruling had indicated that the hearing was scheduled at 10:30 a.m., in Dept 204. The Court advised him that Mr. Madden appeared on the 9:00 a.m. calendar and that trial dates were tentatively set. Mr. Freedman went on to say that the only issue is to determine the status of the commercial building, that would like to have access to the building, and that they want disclosure status of property. The Court advised Mr. Freedman to contact Mr. Madden regarding the trial dates and to advise the clerk to set a status date of April 24, 2013 and vacate the trial date if Mr. Madden is agreeable.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Continued To Mar-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Continued To Mar-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross Madden for and with York Liu. Michael Freedman for and with James Liu. Other Family members were also present.

Mr. Freedman stated that he was not filing anything on Mr. Madden's petition regarding the latter Trust instrument being the operative Trust, but that he filed a petition on the issue of the remains. Counsel did not agree on the form of order. Therefore, Counsel will work on and stipulate to an order with regard to the Petition to Determine Existence of Trust and submit it to the Court. The Family became very emotional when expressing their concerns and when stating what the issues are. Mr. Madden informed the Court that the ashes have been scattered. Mr. Freedman requested confirmation of scattering of the ashes. Mr. Madden also explained that 50% of the property would be distributed to James and 50% to York Liu as Trustee for Samuel Liu, and that the property may need to be sold due to family dynamics. The Court suggested Counsel outline all of the issues that need to be dealt with (ashes, personal property, how the 6th Avenue property is to be handled, etc.). The Petition for Instructions was continued to March 6, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
